political.s: <cite index="1-32,1-33">In 2024, AT&T contributed $5.95 million in campaign contributions and spent $12.05 million on lobbying.</cite> The provided FEC data indicates a right-leaning political stance, with 76.7% of PAC contributions directed to Republicans. <cite index="6-1,6-4">AT&T-affiliated PACs donated at least $312,000 to Texas state lawmakers in June 2021, including a $100,000 donation to Texas Gov. Greg Abbott the same day he called for a special legislative session to pass a widely condemned elections bill.</cite>

charity.s: <cite index="11-1">In 2025, AT&T and the AT&T Foundation contributed more than $72.0 million, including approximately $70.2 million in cash and nearly $1.9 million in in-kind giving.</cite> <cite index="15-3,15-4">The AT&T Foundation's primary giving area is education, with a focus on college and career readiness, and also gives in the fields of digital literacy, climate change, disaster relief and community development.</cite>
charity.focus: Education, digital divide, community development, climate change, disaster relief

environment.s: <cite index="21-1,21-2,21-3">AT&T focuses on reducing greenhouse gas emissions in operations and using connectivity to help customers reduce their environmental footprint, building resilience to extreme weather events, and being a responsible steward of resources.</cite> <cite index="26-1">AT&T was recognized on Newsweek's America's Greenest Companies list for having made impressive efforts to minimize negative environmental impact.</cite> However, comprehensive third-party environmental ratings were not readily available in search results.

labor.s: <cite index="35-11,35-12,35-17">AT&T is the largest union employer in its industry, with nearly 100 years of strong union partnerships and has reached more than 60 full contract agreements with union partners over the past 10 years supporting more than 55,000 frontline employees.</cite> <cite index="40-1,40-4">An AT&T employee filed federal charges against both the company and CWA union officials, alleging that AT&T and CWA officials have colluded to force workers under control of a 'company union' in violation of federal labor law.</cite>

dei.s: <cite index="41-3,41-4,41-7">AT&T is scrapping its diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) programs, with the telecommunications giant writing to the FCC stating it was ending its DEI-related policies and that the company will not use hiring quotas based on race, sex or sexual orientation and had removed all DEI training.</cite> <cite index="45-4,45-5">AT&T has withdrawn financial support from the Trevor Project, a nonprofit focused on suicide prevention for LGBTQ+ youth, and has discontinued its backing of Turn Up the Love, a Pride-themed music event, and stopped providing pronoun pins to employees.</cite>

animals.s: Not applicable for a telecommunications company.

guns.s: Not applicable for a telecommunications company.

privacy.s: <cite index="51-1,51-3">AT&T uses strong safeguards to keep data confidential and secure, and integrates privacy considerations when developing all services and capabilities and conducts annual risk assessments to continuously improve its approach.</cite> <cite index="51-15">AT&T acknowledges that like all companies, it sometimes experiences attempts to gain unauthorized access to customer or employee data.</cite>

execPay.s: <cite index="66-2">AT&T president and CEO John Stankey receives $22.92 million every year which equates to a pay ratio of 416 to 1.</cite> This far exceeds the S&P 500 median CEO-to-worker pay ratio. <cite index="68-2">CEO total yearly compensation is $29.91M, comprised of 8% salary and 92% bonuses, including company stock and options.</cite>

fec.committeeName: INTERNATIONAL ASSOCIATION OF AMUSEMENT PARKS AND ATTRACTIONS (IAAPA PAC)

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
